pageTitle: Pango Diet Maker UX Case Study: Automating Nutrition Planning for Massive Efficiency

sections.title: Pango Diet Maker
sections.subtitle: Transforming a 4-hour manual diet creation process into an automated task completed in under 15 minutes.

visual.altText: A clean, modern interface of the 'Diet Maker' software.

sections.title: Impact by the Numbers
sections.heading: Automation dramatically increased the capacity of the Pango team, turning a major cost center into a scalable service.

projectDescription.label: Pango Nutrition
projectDescription.description: A nutrition service relying on expert nutritionists to create personalized, calculation-intensive diet plans for their growing client base.

projectDescription.label: The Core Problem
projectDescription.value: Diets were created manually in Excel, involving tedious calorie and nutrient checks, which severely capped the business’s ability to take on new clients due to time constraints.

issues.name: Excessive Time Overhead
issues.description: Each personalized diet required approximately 4 hours of manual labor, checking and cross-referencing nutritional data in spreadsheets.

issues.name: Low Scalability
issues.description: With only 10 diets created per week per nutritionist, the business could not grow its client base without hiring more expensive staff.

issues.name: High Risk of Error
issues.description: Manual data entry and calculation in Excel increased the likelihood of calorie or nutrient errors.

visual.altText: A happy nutritionist reviewing a diet plan on a simplified interface.

goals.name: Minimize Diet Creation Time
goals.description: Reduce the time taken to generate a personalized diet plan from hours to minutes.

goals.name: Unlock Scalability and Profit
goals.description: Remove the labor bottleneck to allow the existing team to serve hundreds of clients, driving exponential business growth.

goals.name: Ensure Accuracy and Reliability
goals.description: Automate all calculations to eliminate human error in nutritional planning.

steps.name: User Workflow Analysis
steps.description: Mapped the nutritionist's entire manual process, identifying every step from selecting food items to calculating totals, to ensure comprehensive automation.

steps.name: Database Integration
steps.description: Built a centralized, accurate database of all food items, calorie counts, and nutritional information to replace manual checking.

steps.name: Interface & Logic Design
steps.description: Designed the intuitive Diet Maker interface with logic for user selection, date assignment, and instant calorie calculation.

steps.name: Pre-Made Diet Feature
steps.description: Integrated the ability to assign pre-made diets instantly or use them as a starting template, significantly speeding up customization.

sections.title: The Diet Maker: Efficiency at Your Fingertips

solutions.title: Automated Calculation Engine
solutions.description: The software instantly calculates total calories and nutrients as ingredients are added, removing the 3.5 hours of manual, error-prone spreadsheet work.

solutions.title: Intuitive User-Centric Interface
solutions.description: The simple workflow allows nutritionists to Select User → Select Dates → Assign/Create Diet with minimal clicks, prioritizing speed and clarity.

solutions.title: Template & Custom Creation Flow
solutions.description: Nutritionists can now choose a pre-made template or create a new plan from scratch, both optimized to take under 15 minutes.

solutions.title: Massive Business Scalability
solutions.description: By freeing up the nutritionist's time, the client’s team of 5 is now capable of managing 800 diets per week, unlocking exponential potential revenue and market reach.

takeaways.title: Software Replaces Staffing
takeaways.description: This project highlights that when capacity is the bottleneck, custom automation software can provide a far greater, faster, and cheaper solution than continuous hiring.

takeaways.title: Efficiency Directly Fuels Growth
takeaways.description: The 16X gain in individual productivity directly translated into the potential for 16X business growth, proving automation is a critical strategy for service-based businesses.

takeaways.title: The Focus Must Be on the User's Time
takeaways.description: Designing the 'Diet Maker' was centered on one metric: time. Every interface element was streamlined to minimize the steps and clicks required by the nutritionist, ensuring maximum efficiency.

limitations.name: Project Timeline
limitations.description: The project carried a firm deadline, pushing the team to stay aligned and deliver on time.

limitations.name: Phase 1
limitations.description: Initial planning and research took place, establishing the framework for the project.

limitations.name: Phase 2
limitations.description: Development and implementation, where the core features were built and tested.

limitations.name: Phase 3
limitations.description: Final review and adjustments, ensuring everything met the quality standards before the launch.
